acceptance_criteria: When plan_and_implement runs against a prompt declaring bead_execution_prompt/1, the rendered stage prompt includes a scope-guidance block (analogous to the classification_guidance block for review stages) that instructs the planner to: (a) plan only items within Must-Do Scope and Acceptance Criteria, (b) note items from Explicit Non-Goals as explicitly deferred, (c) note items from Already Planned Elsewhere as deferred with the owning bead ID, (d) note genuinely new out-of-scope items as follow-up candidates rather than absorbing them.
When plan_and_implement runs against a prompt without the canonical contract marker, no scope-guidance block is injected — the stage prompt is identical to the current behavior.

The scope-guidance rendering function lives in the workflow_composition context (e.g., as a new function or in an appropriate existing module like review_classification.rs or a new scope_guidance module).

The guidance block is wired into build_stage_prompt in engine.rs via a conditional check on StageId::PlanAndImplement, following the same pattern as the classification_guidance_block for StageId::Review.

Existing tests continue to pass (cargo test), and new unit tests verify: (a) guidance is rendered when contract marker is present, (b) guidance is empty when contract marker is absent, (c) Already Planned Elsewhere bead IDs are surfaced in the guidance when present.
The guidance text references the exact canonical section names from task_prompt_contract.rs constants, not hardcoded strings.
constraints: Must use the existing optional placeholder mechanism (either classification_guidance or a new optional placeholder) — do not add new required placeholders to the shared stage template manifest.
Must detect the canonical contract via task_prompt_contract::prompt_uses_contract() — do not duplicate contract detection logic.
Must reuse task_prompt_contract::extract_pe_bead_ids() for surfacing planned-elsewhere bead IDs in the guidance — do not re-implement PE extraction.
The rendered guidance must be concise enough to fit within model context alongside the rest of the stage prompt without crowding out the project prompt content.
Follow existing code patterns: Rust module conventions, anyhow error handling, string constants from task_prompt_contract.rs.
goals: Add scope-aware planning guidance to the plan_and_implement stage so that its generated plan respects Must-Do Scope, Explicit Non-Goals, and Already Planned Elsewhere boundaries from the bead execution prompt contract.

When the plan_and_implement stage encounters work that falls outside the current bead's scope, it should note the work as deferred (with a reason: non-goal, planned-elsewhere with bead ID, or new follow-up) rather than including it in the plan.

Maintain backward compatibility: when no canonical bead_execution_prompt contract is detected (non-milestone mode), the plan_and_implement stage must behave exactly as it does today with no additional guidance injected.

Follow the same architectural pattern established by review_classification — a rendering function in the workflow_composition context that conditionally injects a guidance block into the stage template based on prompt contract detection.
non_goals: Modifying the review or final_review classification guidance — those stages already have their own scope routing via review_classification.rs.
Changing the bead execution prompt contract itself (task_prompt_contract.rs sections, marker format, or validation).
Adding scope enforcement to the standalone implementation stage — that stage takes its plan as input and executes it; scope enforcement belongs at plan time.
Adding structured JSON classification fields to the plan_and_implement payload schema — the deferred items should appear as advisory notes in the plan narrative, not as new schema fields.
Changing how the project prompt (prompt.md) is generated or how BeadProjectContext is assembled.
problem_summary: The plan_and_implement stage receives generic task-prompt-contract guidance that lists the canonical sections but lacks actionable scope-enforcement instructions for the planning portion. While review and final_review stages recently gained classification guidance (fix-now, planned-elsewhere, propose-new-bead) to route findings to the right beads, plan_and_implement has no equivalent mechanism. This means the stage can silently absorb out-of-scope work into its plan — items that belong to other beads, are explicit non-goals, or are already planned elsewhere — leading to scope creep, duplicated effort across beads, and inflated diffs that reviewers must untangle downstream.
risks_or_open_questions: Should the scope guidance reuse the existing classification_guidance optional placeholder slot, or should a new optional placeholder (e.g., scope_guidance) be added to STAGE_OPTIONAL? Reusing classification_guidance is simpler but conflates two distinct concerns; a new placeholder is cleaner but adds to the manifest.

Should the plan_and_implement stage also receive the classification_guidance for its review-like aspects (e.g., noting findings during implementation), or should scope guidance be kept separate from finding classification?

The deferred-items instruction is advisory (natural language in the plan narrative). If the model ignores scope guidance and plans out-of-scope work anyway, the downstream review stage will still catch it via classification. Is advisory-only sufficient, or should a structured deferred_items field be added to the ExecutionPayload schema as a future follow-up?

Should the same scope guidance also apply to the standalone Planning stage (StageId::Planning) in standard flow, or only to plan_and_implement in the minimal flow? The planning stage has similar scope-creep risk.
